title: Package sprawl control review
scope: Overlapping commands, verbosity/profile modes, scaffolds, and documentation authority surfaces
goal: Identify the package-level control work needed to prevent overlapping commands, profiles, scaffolds, and documents from increasing architectural sprawl.
non_goals: Do not implement the cleanup in this review pass.
Do not promote every conceptual concern into active work without prioritisation.

review_mode.review question: Do current package surfaces make the intended authority path obvious, or do overlapping commands, modes, scaffolds, and docs still require agents to infer which one matters?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: AGENTS.md routed start output, active Planning summary, .agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md, CLI command contracts, generated TypeScript command package, Planning/Memory README authority sections, review and execplan docs.

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --target . --task "Review how to continuously prevent overlapping commands, profiles, scaffolds, and documents from increasing package sprawl" --format json --select immediate_next_allowed_action,workflow_sufficiency,skill_routing,task_intent; uv run agentic-workspace summary --target . --format json --select planning_surface_health,todo.active_items,warnings,warning_count; rg over workflow, docs, CLI contracts, generated TypeScript command package, Planning/Memory docs, and scaffold surfaces.

findings.title: Profile vocabulary remains as an overlapping output model
findings.summary: The selector-first direction is partially landed, but command contracts and generated TypeScript surfaces still expose tiny/compact/full/profile language across many routine commands.
findings.evidence: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/cli_commands.json declares --profile for modules, summary, compatibility, implement, defaults, proof, config, report, preflight, status, and doctor; generated/typescript/workspace-cli/src/commandPackage.ts mirrors the same profile options; packages/planning/README.md still documents tiny, compact, and full profiles for active planning.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents must choose between profile escalation and field selection, which reintroduces broad reads and makes the intended output contract ambiguous.
findings.suggested action: Promote a compatibility-deprecation lane: make selector-first the single ordinary detail mechanism, keep --verbose as the broad diagnostic affordance, and either remove --profile from public docs/contracts or mark it compatibility-only with tests that default outputs advertise selectors first.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or active execplan after the current generated-CLI stage is shaped
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when touching generated CLI contracts or any command output contract next.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink to a pointer to the command-contract cleanup issue and tests.

findings.title: Routing authority is spread across too many surfaces
findings.summary: The intended authority path exists, but similar routing guidance appears in WORKFLOW, cold-start docs, which-package, Planning README, Memory README, compact-contract docs, and command outputs.
findings.evidence: .agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md names start, implement --changed, preflight, summary, config, defaults, skills, and proof; docs/which-package.md points to defaults sections for preset and operating questions; packages/planning/README.md and packages/memory/README.md restate selective adoption, startup, and ownership boundaries; .agentic-workspace/docs/cold-start-protocol.md also names startup commands.
findings.risk if unchanged: Every helpful explanation can become a second source of truth. Agents may read more than needed or follow a stale doc rather than the current command output.
findings.suggested action: Create a small authority-ladder contract for operational surfaces: one canonical route per question, one owning command, one owning doc, and explicit demotion of all other prose to explanatory/non-authoritative status. Add a review/check that flags docs introducing new first-step routes without updating the ladder.
findings.promotion trigger: Promote before the next broad documentation or command-surface rewrite.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink to the authority-ladder doc/check and any issue link.

findings.title: Scaffolds can carry stale or contradictory mode residue
findings.summary: A generated plan scaffold can contain mode-specific fields that later become wrong when the plan changes shape, leaving hidden contradictory instructions inside the same canonical artifact.
findings.evidence: .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/generated-cli-next-stage.plan.json was initially created prep-only, then converted to an implementation plan; rg still surfaced prep-only residue under control gate text after the main planning_mode fields were changed. The summary was clean because canonical projections agreed, but deeper raw scaffold content still carried stale halt wording.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents following raw plan detail may obey outdated scaffold residue even when compact projections say something else. This increases architectural complexity because agents must know which fields are authoritative inside a single artifact.
findings.suggested action: Add scaffold hygiene work: either generate smaller shape-specific plan records, provide package commands to convert prep-only to implementation mode, or add a planning health check that detects contradictory mode residue outside canonical projection fields.
findings.promotion target: active issue if repeated during generated-CLI work; otherwise roadmap
findings.promotion trigger: Promote immediately if another plan conversion or weak-agent eval hits stale scaffold instructions.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink to the regression check and command behavior.

recommendation.promote: Promote profile deprecation and scaffold hygiene soon; both are directly connected to current generated-CLI and planning reliability work.
recommendation.defer: Defer the broader authority-ladder doc/check until after the current generated-CLI slice is shaped, unless documentation work starts first.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss these findings; they match repeated dogfooding friction and the user's stated concern.

retention.proof surface: routed issues, planning state, docs, checks, Memory, or a compact retained review stub

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace start --target . --task "Review how to continuously prevent overlapping commands, profiles, scaffolds, and documents from increasing package sprawl" --format json --select immediate_next_allowed_action,workflow_sufficiency,skill_routing,task_intent
rg -n -- "--profile|profile|--verbose|--select" src/agentic_workspace packages/memory packages/planning generated tools -g "*.py" -g "*.json"
drift_log: 2026-05-10: Captured bounded contract-integrity findings for package sprawl control.
